page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn7i-c16g1.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs operating at 2.9 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture with 16 physical cores and a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance is equipped with 60.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via a single NVIDIA A10 GPU from the Ampere architecture, which includes 24 GB of dedicated VRAM. It lacks local storage, requiring network-attached storage options, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average-tier CPU and memory capabilities alongside top-tier GPU-driven performance. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1584.0 and a multi-core score of 9350.0, while the Passmark CPU score is 21025.87. Memory read bandwidth is measured at 855.56 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 58.64 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The standout performance is in LLM inference, where the NVIDIA A10 GPU achieves top-tier results, processing 9711.75 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M model and 2480.56 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high resource density for GPU-accelerated workloads without the overhead of larger multi-GPU configurations. The primary tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, meaning all disk operations must rely on network storage, which can impact I/O-heavy workloads. Given the hardware profile and benchmark results, this instance is highly suited for small-to-medium LLM inference, ray tracing, image processing, and GPU-accelerated compute tasks, while also handling general-purpose workloads like static web serving and database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn7i-c16g1.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for compute-intensive and graphics-heavy workloads. It features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.9 GHz, 60.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA A10 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights average-tier CPU performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9350.0, but top-tier LLM inference capabilities, achieving 2480.56 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model. This hardware configuration makes the instance highly efficient for small-to-medium machine learning inference, ray tracing, and image processing, balancing dedicated CPU power with Ampere-generation GPU acceleration.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn7i-c16g1.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 60 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A10 GPU with 24 GB VRAM, optimized for AI inference and graphics.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn7i-c16g1.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 60 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A10 GPU for top-tier LLM inference and graphics.
tagline: Accelerate machine learning inference and graphics workloads with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and an NVIDIA Ampere A10 GPU.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs at 2.9 GHz
NVIDIA A10 GPU with 24 GB VRAM for accelerated computing
60.0 GB system memory (3.75 GB per core)
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small and medium models
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
